member.name: Naushabah Khan
member.synopsis: Naushabah Khan is the Labour MP for Gillingham and Rainham , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Written question
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment his department has made of trends in the level of child poverty in the Gillingham and Rainham constituency; and what steps his department is taking to reduce child poverty in that area.

writtenQuestions.heading: Surgical Mesh Implants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what (a) mental health and (b) long-term support is available for women living with the consequences of pelvic mesh injuries.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government extends its deepest sympathies to all those affected by pelvic mesh, and recognises the profound, life‑changing impact these harms have had on individuals and their families. We know that for many, the consequences are ongoing and deeply fe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Russia: Shipping
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what plans the Government has to increase the frequency of interdiction operations against Russian shadow fleet vessels.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence continuously monitors maritime activity in the UK waters. Any enforcement action is considered on a case‑by‑case basis. The recent interdiction of Russian shadow fleet vessel SMYRTOS shows that the UK is ready to take action to enf...

writtenQuestions.heading: Anti-social Behaviour: Medway
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps the Government is taking to increase police presence and reduce anti-social behaviour in town centres in Medway.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through our Crime and Policing Act, we are rolling out new enhanced powers to enable local agencies to tackle antisocial behaviour offenders. And our Neighbourhood Policing Guarantee has ensured that every police force in England and Wales now has a dedic...

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Housing: Medway
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what proportion of the £2.5 billion social housing loan fund for 2026 to 2030 will benefit housing providers in Medway; and what steps are being taken to address homelessness in
writtenQuestions.answerText: To support the delivery of new social and affordable housing, the government will make available £2.5 billion in low interest loans to Private Registered Providers, with £1 billion available outside of London. The loans will be made available via a compet...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Gillingham and Rainham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to ensure that the transfer of Southeastern to public ownership will improve service reliability at Gillingham and Rainham stations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Southeastern has been in public ownership since Oct 2021. Overall, Southeastern is performing well above the industry average for both reliability and punctuality. In fact, they are currently ranked fourth out of 14 DfT managed train operators for both Ca...

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what the Government's timetable is for increasing defence spending to meet NATO commitments; and whether he is satisfied that current funding levels are sufficient to deter Russian aggression and maintain the UK'
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government remains committed to spending 3.5% of GDP on defence by 2035, as agreed at the NATO summit last year.As the third-largest financial contributor, and the only European Ally to commit our full nuclear deterrent to NATO, the United Kingdom alr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Gillingham and Rainham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he plans to take steps to (a) improve GP access and (b) help reduce waiting times at Medway Maritime Hospital's emergency department in Gillingham and Rainham constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are improving capacity in general practices (GPs). We have invested £160 million into the Additional Roles Reimbursement Scheme to support the recruitment of over 2,000 more GPs into primary care networks across England, helping to increase appointment...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Alcoholic Drinks: Delivery Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment the Government has made of calls to ban the sale and delivery of alcohol through online delivery apps; and what steps are being taken to strengthen age verification requirements and pr
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Licensing Act 2003 provides the legal framework for the sale and supply of alcohol in England and Wales. It is already an offence, under section 141 of the Act, for a person to knowingly sell or attempt to sell alcohol to a person who is drunk or unde...

writtenQuestions.heading: Curriculum: Discrimination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has plans to introduce (a) misogyny and (b) the treatment of women onto the national curriculum in schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer my hon. Friend, the Member for Gillingham and Rainham to the answer of 1 April 2025 to Question 40591.

writtenQuestions.heading: Homes for Ukraine Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many applicants are waiting for their applications to be processed on the Homes for Ukraine scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on outstanding applications are not routinely published by the Home Office. However, the Hon Member can be assured that ordinary applications made under the Homes for Ukraine scheme are currently being processed within the published service standards...

writtenQuestions.heading: Homes for Ukraine Scheme: Visas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to tackle the backlog of visa applications under the Homes for Ukraine scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on outstanding applications are not routinely published by the Home Office. However, the Hon Member can be assured that ordinary applications made under the Homes for Ukraine scheme are currently being processed within the published service standards...

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he is taking steps to ensure constituents are financially supported when requesting a letter from their GP.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are some medical evidence letters, certificates or reports that general practices (GPs) may charge for, and others that they must not charge patients for. The legislation that sets this out is The National Health Service (General Medical Services Co...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
